metadata.title: Sacituzumab Govitecan Therapy
metadata.indication: Treatment of eligible adults with unresectable locally advanced or metastatic triple-negative breast cancer in accordance with NCCP regimen 00794.
metadata.partial_assessment_note: Only entered values are assessed; omitted clinical domains remain explicitly unassessed.

clinical_governance.prescriptive_authority: Treatment plan must be initiated by a Consultant Medical Oncologist.
clinical_governance.dose_modification_note: Any dose modification should be discussed with a Consultant.
clinical_governance.disclaimer: Decision-support encoding of the current NCCP source. It does not replace the NCCP regimen, clinical judgement, local policy, prescribing review or pharmacy verification.

febrile_neutropenia_grade34.label: Febrile neutropenia meeting protocol criteria
febrile_neutropenia_grade34.help_text: Assess ANC together with a single oral temperature >38.3°C, temperature ≥38.0°C sustained for more than 1 hour, and/or concurrent clinically significant infection. Treat as an urgent oncology complication.

nonhaem_grade.label: Worst current non-haematological toxicity (CTCAE)

options.label: Grade 0 — no toxicity
options.description: No clinical or laboratory evidence of non-haematological toxicity.

options.label: Grade 1 — mild
options.description: Mild or asymptomatic; clinical observation or diagnostic review only; intervention generally not indicated.

options.label: Grade 2 — moderate
options.description: Moderate symptoms or abnormality; minimal, local or non-invasive intervention may be indicated; limits age-appropriate instrumental activities of daily living.

options.label: Grade 3 — severe
options.description: Severe or medically significant but not immediately life-threatening; hospitalisation or prolongation of hospitalisation may be indicated; disabling; limits self-care activities of daily living.

nonhaem_grade.assessment_guidance: Name the exact non-haematological adverse event first and apply that event's own CTCAE v5.0 criterion. The general Grade 1-4 severity anchors are a screening aid only and are not a substitute for the named term.

uncontrolled_nvd.label: Severe nausea, vomiting or diarrhoea despite optimal treatment
uncontrolled_nvd.help_text: For diarrhoea, document stools above baseline, nocturnal stools, continence, hydration, IV fluids and admission. For nausea/vomiting, assess oral intake, weight, dehydration, IV fluids/tube feeding and hospitalisation.

not_recovered_within_3_weeks.label: Grade 3–4 toxicity not recovered to ≤grade 1 within 3 weeks

output_templates.proceed: No encoded contraindication or hold criterion was triggered. Complete normal prescribing and pharmacy checks.
output_templates.coverage_gap: Only entered values were assessed; unentered clinically relevant fields remain unassessed.

action.recommendation: Do not administer during pregnancy or breastfeeding.

rules.explanation: Do not administer during pregnancy or breastfeeding.

action.recommendation: Withhold and obtain consultant review for active uncontrolled infection.

rules.explanation: Withhold and obtain consultant review for active uncontrolled infection.

action.recommendation: Consider a lower starting dose and dose escalation according to cycle-1 toxicity.

rules.explanation: Consider a lower starting dose and dose escalation according to cycle-1 toxicity.

action.recommendation: Do not administer on Day 1 if ANC <1.5 ×10⁹/L.

rules.explanation: Do not administer on Day 1 if ANC <1.5 ×10⁹/L.

action.recommendation: Do not administer on Day 8 if ANC <1.0 ×10⁹/L.

rules.explanation: Do not administer on Day 8 if ANC <1.0 ×10⁹/L.

action.recommendation: Permanently discontinue for a life-threatening infusion-related reaction.

rules.explanation: Permanently discontinue for a life-threatening infusion-related reaction.

action.recommendation: Discontinue when grade 3–4 neutropenia delays dosing beyond 3 weeks; administer G-CSF as clinically indicated.

rules.explanation: Discontinue when grade 3–4 neutropenia delays dosing beyond 3 weeks; administer G-CSF as clinically indicated.

action.recommendation: Fourth severe-neutropenia occurrence: discontinue and administer G-CSF as clinically indicated.

rules.explanation: Fourth severe-neutropenia occurrence: discontinue and administer G-CSF as clinically indicated.

action.recommendation: Third severe-neutropenia occurrence: reduce dose by 50% and administer G-CSF.

rules.explanation: Third severe-neutropenia occurrence: reduce dose by 50% and administer G-CSF.

action.recommendation: Second severe-neutropenia occurrence: reduce dose by 25% and administer G-CSF.

rules.explanation: Second severe-neutropenia occurrence: reduce dose by 25% and administer G-CSF.

action.recommendation: First severe-neutropenia occurrence: administer G-CSF as soon as clinically indicated.

rules.explanation: First severe-neutropenia occurrence: administer G-CSF as soon as clinically indicated.

action.recommendation: Third severe non-neutropenic toxicity occurrence: discontinue treatment.

rules.explanation: Third severe non-neutropenic toxicity occurrence: discontinue treatment.

action.recommendation: Severe non-neutropenic toxicity: reduce dose by 25% for the first or second occurrence.

rules.explanation: Severe non-neutropenic toxicity: reduce dose by 25% for the first or second occurrence.

action.recommendation: Discontinue if grade 3–4 toxicity does not recover to ≤grade 1 within 3 weeks.

rules.explanation: Discontinue if grade 3–4 toxicity does not recover to ≤grade 1 within 3 weeks.

action.recommendation: For uncontrolled grade 3–4 nausea, vomiting or diarrhoea, apply the occurrence-based 25% dose-reduction pathway.

rules.explanation: For uncontrolled grade 3–4 nausea, vomiting or diarrhoea, apply the occurrence-based 25% dose-reduction pathway.

supportive_care.supportive_medications_label: CUH high-risk prescription sheet (2021; pharmacy reconciliation required)
premedication_summary: Antiemetic prophylaxis appropriate to high emetogenic risk
Consider corticosteroid according to local infusion-reaction policy
other_supportive_care: Early antidiarrhoeal treatment and hydration advice
G-CSF according to severe-neutropenia history and protocol pathway
Observe during each infusion and for at least 30 minutes afterwards
supportive_care.mapping_basis: The active ADC is high emetogenic risk in NCCP V6 Table 7.

ctcae_standard.reference: CTCAE grading as cited by the NCCP source protocol
ctcae_standard.display_requirement: Every grade-based input includes toxicity-specific criteria and practical assessment guidance. Do not select a grade from the number alone.
